---
title: "LRPDID01: Waitlist-crossover arm-by-wave sensitivity for word reading (EWRSWR) (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Substantially revised by a LLM-based AI tool (Codex/GPT-5).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

**In plain terms.** LRPDID01 asks how the immediate and waitlist arms differ in word reading at t1, t2 and t3 without forcing the randomised t2 contrast to equal post-crossover catch-up. It is a longitudinal sensitivity analysis alongside LRPITT10, not an independent replication.

::: {.callout-note title="What this estimand is, and is not"}

`arm_gap_t1` checks pre-treatment balance. `tau_t2`, the immediate-minus-waitlist contrast at t2, is randomised and may be interpreted causally under the trial assumptions. At t3 both arms have received intervention, but the immediate arm has a longer and different exposure history; `arm_gap_t3` and `delta_crossover = tau_t2 - arm_gap_t3` are therefore post-crossover associations. Interpreting either as a treatment effect would additionally require no carryover, cumulative-dose, block-content or diminishing-return differences.

No period-start outcome enters the model: t2 is already treatment-affected for the immediate arm when it becomes the P2 baseline. The child random intercept partially pools stable differences; it does not make every child a fixed-effect control.

:::

::: {.callout-note title="Sign convention and the ceiling"}

For the randomised t2 arm contrast, positive means that the immediate arm scored higher than the waitlist arm. The Beta-Binomial logit respects the bounded 0–79 score and accommodates overdispersion; it does not itself license a causal interpretation of the post-crossover contrasts.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_{i,t} = \alpha + \beta_{\text{period},t}
           + \text{arm\_gap}_{t}\,G_i
           + \gamma_A\,A_{t1,\text{std},i} + u_{\text{child},i},
$$

with a Beta-Binomial likelihood on the t1/t2/t3 count, $N = 79$, $G_i=1$ for the immediate arm, $\beta_{\text{period},t1}=0$, and $\text{arm\_gap}_{t}=(\text{arm\_gap}_{t1},\tau_{t2},\text{arm\_gap}_{t3})$. The intercept is expressed as a learned offset around the observed-t1 logit anchor. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_did.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
